title: Here’s what happened in crypto today

title: CFTC officials who questioned prediction markets were suspended: NYT
summary: A New York Times investigation found that senior CFTC officials who raised concerns about Polymarket, Crypto.com and Gemini were suspended and pushed out.

summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said there may be “some good news” regarding the blocked Strait of Hormuz in the coming hours, as Iran and Washington press ahead with peace negotiations.

title: Firefox's Big Redesign Gives You a Button to Kill All the AI
summary: Project Nova is coming later this year with a cleaner look, compact mode, and a toggle to make AI features disappear entirely.

title: Bitcoin Dives Below $75K for First Time in a Month as Crypto Liquidations Near $1 Billion
summary: Bitcoin touched its lowest price in a month overnight following an awful week for ETFs, which shed over $1.25 billion this week.

title: ECB warns EU finance ministers that easing euro stablecoin rules would weaken banks: Reuters
summary: ECB President Christine Lagarde warned the changes would destabilize bank funding and weaken interest-rate transmission.

title: FTX’s former law firm, auditor agree to pay $66 million to settle customer claims over fraud
summary: The law firm, Fenwick & West, denied wrongdoing and still faces a separate $525 million suit in Washington that is not covered by Friday's deal.
